text.KJV: 17:10 And there are seuen Kings, fiue are fallen, and one is, and the other is not yet come: and when he commeth, he must continue a short space.
text.DRV: 17:10 Five are fallen, one is, and another is not yet come: and when he shall come, he must tarry a short time.
text.NABRE: 17:10 five have already fallen, one still lives, and the last has not yet come, and when he comes he must remain only a short while.

text.NRSV: As for the beast that was and is not, it is an eighth but it belongs to the seven, and it goes to destruction.
text.Rotherham: and the wild-beast which was and is not. And he, is an eighth, and is, of the seven,—and, into destruction, goeth away.
text.KJV: 17:11 And the beast that was, and is not, euen he is the eighth, & is of the seuen, and goeth into perdition.
text.DRV: 17:11 And the beast which was, and is not: the same also is the eight, and is of the seven, & goeth into destruction.
text.NABRE: 17:11 The beast that existed once but exists no longer is an eighth king, but really belongs to the seven and is headed for destruction.

text.NRSV: And the ten horns that you saw are ten kings who have not yet received a kingdom, but they are to receive authority as kings for one hour, together with the beast.
text.Rotherham: And, the ten horns which thou sawest, are, ten kings,—who, indeed, have not received, sovereignty, as yet, but, authority, as kings, for one hour, shall receive, with the wild-beast.
text.KJV: 17:12 And the tenne hornes which thou sawest, are ten kings, which haue receiued no kingdom as yet: but receiue power as kings one houre with the beast.
text.DRV: 17:12 And the ten horns which thou sawest, are ten kings, which have not yet received kingdom, but shall receive power as kings one hour after the beast.
text.NABRE: 17:12 The ten horns that you saw represent ten kings who have not yet been crowned; they will receive royal authority along with the beast for one hour.

text.NRSV: These are united in yielding their power and authority to the beast;
text.Rotherham: These, have, one mind, and, their power and authority, unto the wild-beast, they give.
text.KJV: 17:13 These haue one minde, and shall giue their power and strength vnto the beast.
text.DRV: 17:13 These have one counsel and force: and their power they shall deliver to the beast.
text.NABRE: 17:13 They are of one mind and will give their power and authority to the beast.

text.NRSV: they will make war on the Lamb, and the Lamb will conquer them, for he is Lord of lords and King of kings, and those with him are called and chosen and faithful.”
text.Rotherham: These, with the Lamb, will make war; and, the Lamb, will overcome them, because he is, Lord of lords, and King of kings,—and, they who are with him, are called and chosen and faithful.
text.KJV: 17:14 These shal make warre with the Lambe, and the Lambe shal ouercome them: For he is Lord of Lords, and King of kings, and they that are with him, are called, & chosen, and faithfull.
text.DRV: 17:14 These shall fight with the Lamb, and the Lamb shall overcome them, because he is Lord of lords, and King of kings, and they that are with him, called, and elect, and faithful.
text.NABRE: 17:14 They will fight with the Lamb, but the Lamb will conquer them, for he is Lord of lords and king of kings, and those with him are called, chosen, and faithful.”

text.NRSV: And he said to me, “The waters that you saw, where the whore is seated, are peoples and multitudes and nations and languages.
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto me—The waters which thou sawest, where the harlot sitteth, are, peoples and multitudes, and nations and tongues.
text.KJV: 17:15 And he saith vnto me, The waters which thou sawest, where the whore sitteth, are peoples, and multitudes, and nations, and tongues.
text.DRV: 17:15 And he said to me: The waters which thou sawest where the harlot sitteth, are peoples, and nations, and tongues.
text.NABRE: 17:15 Then he said to me, “The waters that you saw where the harlot lives represent large numbers of peoples, nations, and tongues.

text.NRSV: And the ten horns that you saw, they and the beast will hate the whore; they will make her desolate and naked; they will devour her flesh and burn her up with fire.
text.Rotherham: And the ten horns which thou sawest, and the wild-beast, these, shall hate the harlot, and, desolate, shall make her, and naked, and, her flesh, shall they eat, and, herself, shall they burn up with fire.
text.KJV: 17:16 And the ten hornes which thou sawest vpon the beast, these shall hate the whore, and shall make her desolate, and naked, and shall eate her flesh, and burne her with fire.
text.DRV: 17:16 And the ten horns which thou sawest in the beast: these shall hate the harlot, and shall make her desolate and naked, and shall eat her flesh, and her they shall burn with fire.
text.NABRE: 17:16 The ten horns that you saw and the beast will hate the harlot; they will leave her desolate and naked; they will eat her flesh and consume her with fire.

text.NRSV: For God has put it into their hearts to carry out his purpose by agreeing to give their kingdom to the beast, until the words of God will be fulfilled.
text.Rotherham: For, God, hath put into their hearts, to do his mind, and to do one mind,—and to give their sovereignty unto the wild-beast, until the words of God shall be completed.
text.KJV: 17:17 For God hath put in their hearts to fulfill his will, and to agree, and giue their kingdome vnto the beast, vntil the words of God shall be fulfilled.
text.DRV: 17:17 For God hath given into their hearts, to do that which pleaseth him: that they give their kingdom to the beast, till the words of God be consummate.
text.NABRE: 17:17 For God has put it into their minds to carry out his purpose and to make them come to an agreement to give their kingdom to the beast until the words of God are accomplished.

text.NRSV: The woman whom you saw is the great city that rules over the kings of the earth.”
text.Rotherham: And, the woman whom thou sawest, is the great city, which hath sovereignty over the kings of the earth.
text.KJV: 17:18 And the woman which thou sawest, is that great Citie which reigneth ouer the kings of the earth.
text.DRV: 17:18 And the woman which thou sawest: is the great city, which hath kingdom over the kings of the earth.
text.NABRE: 17:18 The woman whom you saw represents the great city that has sovereignty over the kings of the earth.”

text.NRSV: After this I saw another angel coming down from heaven, having great authority; and the earth was made bright with his splendor.
text.Rotherham: After these things, I saw another messenger, coming down out of heaven,—having great authority; and, the earth, was illumined with his glory.
text.KJV: 18:1 And after these things, I saw another Angel come downe from heauen, hauing great power, and the earth was lightened with his glory.
text.DRV: 18:1 And after these things I saw another Angel coming down from heaven, having great power: and the earth was illuminated of his glory.
text.NABRE: 18:1 Chapter 18 - The Fall of Babylon. After this I saw another angel coming down from heaven, having great authority, and the earth became illumined by his splendor.

text.NRSV: He called out with a mighty voice, “Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great! It has become a dwelling place of demons, a haunt of every foul spirit, a haunt of every foul bird, a haunt of every foul and hateful beast.
text.Rotherham: And he cried out, with a mighty voice, saying—Fallen! fallen! is Babylon the Great, and hath become a habitation of demons, and a prison of every impure spirit, and a prison of every impure and hated bird;
text.KJV: 18:2 And he cryed mightily with a strōg voyce, saying, Babylon the great is fallen, is fallen, and is become the habitation of deuils, and the hold of euery foule spirit, and a cage of euery vncleane and hatefull bird:
text.DRV: 18:2 And he cried out in force, saying: Fallen fallen is Babylon the great: and it is become the habitation of devils, and the custody of every unclean spirit, & the custody of every unclean & hateful bird:
text.NABRE: 18:2 He cried out in a mighty voice: “Fallen, fallen is Babylon the great. She has become a haunt for demons. She is a cage for every unclean spirit, a cage for every unclean bird, [a cage for every unclean] and disgusting [beast].

text.NRSV: For all nations have drunk of the wine of the wrath of her fornication, and the kings of the earth have committed fornication with her, and the merchants of the earth have grown rich from the power of her luxury.”
text.Rotherham: Because, by reason [of the wine] of the wrath of her lewdness, have all the nations fallen, and, the kings of the earth, with her, did commit lewdness, and, the merchants of the earth, by reason of the power of her wantonness, waxed rich.
text.KJV: 18:3 For all nations haue drunke of the wine of the wrath of her fornication, and the Kings of the earth haue committed fornication with her, & the Merchants of the earth are waxed rich thorow the abundance of her delicacies.
text.DRV: 18:3 because all nations have drunk of the wine of the wrath of her fornication: and the kings of the earth have fornicated with her: and the merchants of the earth were made rich by the virtue of her delicacies.
text.NABRE: 18:3 For all the nations have drunk the wine of her licentious passion. The kings of the earth had intercourse with her, and the merchants of the earth grew rich from her drive for luxury.”

text.NRSV: Then I heard another voice from heaven saying, “Come out of her, my people, so that you do not take part in her sins, and so that you do not share in her plagues;
text.Rotherham: And I heard another voice out of heaven, saying—Come forth, my people, out of her,—that ye may have no fellowship with her sins, and, of her plagues, that ye may not receive;
text.KJV: 18:4 And I heard another voice from heauen, saying, Come out of her, my people, that yee be not partakers of her sinnes, and that yee receiue not of her plagues:
text.DRV: 18:4 And I heard another voice from heaven, saying: Go out from her my people: that you be not partakers of her sins, and receive not of her plagues.
text.NABRE: 18:4 Then I heard another voice from heaven say: “Depart from her, my people, so as not to take part in her sins and receive a share in her plagues,

text.NRSV: for her sins are heaped high as heaven, and God has remembered her iniquities.
text.Rotherham: because her sins were joined together as far as heaven, and God hath remembered her unrighteous deeds.
text.KJV: 18:5 For her sinnes haue reached vnto heauen, and God hath remembred her iniquities.
text.DRV: 18:5 Because her sins are come even to heaven, and God hath remembered her iniquities.
text.NABRE: 18:5 for her sins are piled up to the sky, and God remembers her crimes.

text.NRSV: Render to her as she herself has rendered, and repay her double for her deeds; mix a double draught for her in the cup she mixed.
text.Rotherham: Render ye unto her, as, she also, rendered, and double [the] double, according to her works,—in the cup wherein she mixed, mix, unto her, double,—
text.KJV: 18:6 Reward her euen as she rewarded you, and double vnto her double according to her works: in the cup which she hath filled, fill to her double.
text.DRV: 18:6 Render to her as she also hath rendered to you: and double ye double according to her works: In the cup wherein she hath mingled, mingle ye double unto her.
text.NABRE: 18:6 Pay her back as she has paid others. Pay her back double for her deeds. Into her cup pour double what she poured.

text.NRSV: As she glorified herself and lived luxuriously, so give her a like measure of torment and grief. Since in her heart she says, ‘I rule as a queen; I am no widow, and I will never see grief,’
text.Rotherham: As much as she glorified herself, and waxed wanton, so much give, unto her, torment and grief:—because, in her heart, she saith—I sit a Queen, and, widow, am I not, and, grief, in nowise shall I see!
text.KJV: 18:7 How much she hath glorified her selfe, and liued deliciously, so much torment and sorrow giue her: for she saith in her heart, I sit a Queene, and am no widow, and shall see no sorrow.
text.DRV: 18:7 As much as she hath glorified herself, & hath been in delicacies, so much give her torment and mourning: because she saith in her heart, I sit a queen, and widow I am not, and mourning I shall not see.
text.NABRE: 18:7 To the measure of her boasting and wantonness repay her in torment and grief; for she said to herself, ‘I sit enthroned as queen; I am no widow, and I will never know grief.’

text.NRSV: therefore her plagues will come in a single day— pestilence and mourning and famine— and she will be burned with fire; for mighty is the Lord God who judges her.”
text.Rotherham: Therefore, in one day, shall have come her plagues,—death and grief and famine; and, with fire, shall she be burned up;—because, mighty, is the [Lord] God who hath judged her.
text.KJV: 18:8 Therefore shall her plagues come in one day, death, and mourning, and famine, and she shall bee vtterly burnt with fire, for strong is the Lord God, who iudgeth her.
text.DRV: 18:8 Therefore in one day shall her plagues come, death, and mourning, and famine, & with fire she shall be burnt: because God is strong that shall judge her.
text.NABRE: 18:8 Therefore, her plagues will come in one day, pestilence, grief, and famine; she will be consumed by fire. For mighty is the Lord God who judges her.”

text.NRSV: And the kings of the earth, who committed fornication with her and lived in luxury with her, will weep and wail over her when they see the smoke of her burning;
text.Rotherham: And they shall weep and wail over her—shall the kings of the earth who, with her, committed lewdness and waxed wanton,—as soon as they see the smoke of her burning,—
text.KJV: 18:9 And the Kings of the earth, who haue committed fornication, and liued deliciously with her, shall bewaile her and lament for her, when they shall see the smoke of her burning:
text.DRV: 18:9 And the kings of the earth, which have fornicated with her, & have lived in delicacies, shall weep, and bewail themselves upon her, when they shall see the smoke of her burning:
text.NABRE: 18:9 The kings of the earth who had intercourse with her in their wantonness will weep and mourn over her when they see the smoke of her pyre.

text.NRSV: they will stand far off, in fear of her torment, and say, “Alas, alas, the great city, Babylon, the mighty city! For in one hour your judgment has come.”
text.Rotherham: afar off, standing, because of their fear of her torment, saying—Alas! alas! the great city! Babylon, the mighty city! That, in one hour, hath come thy judgment.
text.KJV: 18:10 Standing afarre off for the feare of her torment, saying, Alas, alas, that great citie Babylon, that mighty citie: for in one houre is thy iudgement come.
text.DRV: 18:10 standing far off for the fear of her torments, saying: Woe, woe, that great city Babylon, that strong city: because in one hour is thy judgement come.
text.NABRE: 18:10 They will keep their distance for fear of the torment inflicted on her, and they will say: “Alas, alas, great city, Babylon, mighty city. In one hour your judgment has come.”

text.NRSV: And the merchants of the earth weep and mourn for her, since no one buys their cargo anymore,
text.Rotherham: And, the merchants of the earth, weep and grieve over her, because, their cargo, no one, buyeth any more:
text.KJV: 18:11 And the Merchants of the earth shall weepe and mourne ouer her, for no man buyeth their merchandise any more.
text.DRV: 18:11 And the merchants of the earth shall weep, & mourn upon her: because no man shall buy their merchandise any more,
text.NABRE: 18:11 The merchants of the earth will weep and mourn for her, because there will be no more markets for their cargo:

text.NRSV: cargo of gold, silver, jewels and pearls, fine linen, purple, silk and scarlet, all kinds of scented wood, all articles of ivory, all articles of costly wood, bronze, iron, and marble,
text.Rotherham: cargo of gold, and silver, and precious stone, and pearls, and fine linen, and purple, and silk, and scarlet, and all thyine wood, and every article of ivory, and every article of wood most precious, and of copper, and of iron, and of marble,
text.KJV: 18:12 The merchandise of gold, and siluer, and pretious stones, and of pearles, and fine linnen, and purple, and silke, and scarlet, and all Thine wood, and all maner vessels of Yuorie, and all maner vessels of most precious wood, and of brasse, and iron, and marble,
text.DRV: 18:12 merchandise of gold and silver and precious stone: and of pearl, and fine linen, and purple, and silk, & scarlet and all Thyine wood, and all vessels of ivory, and all vessels of precious stone and of brass and iron and marble,
text.NABRE: 18:12 their cargo of gold, silver, precious stones, and pearls; fine linen, purple silk, and scarlet cloth; fragrant wood of every kind, all articles of ivory and all articles of the most expensive wood, bronze, iron, and marble;

text.NRSV: cinnamon, spice, incense, myrrh, frankincense, wine, olive oil, choice flour and wheat, cattle and sheep, horses and chariots, and slaves—and human lives.
text.Rotherham: and cinnamon, and spice, and incense, and unguent, and frankincense, and wine, and oil, and fine flour, and wheat, and cattle, and sheep, and [cargo] of horses, and of chariots, and of bodies, and lives of men.
text.KJV: 18:13 And Cynamome, and odours, and ointments, and frankincense, & wine, and oile, and fine floure, and wheat, and beasts, and sheepe, and horses, and chariots, and slaues, and soules of men.
text.DRV: 18:13 and cinnamon, and of odours, and ointment, and frankincense, and wine, and oil, and flour, & wheat, and beasts, and sheep, and horses, and chariots, & slaves, and souls of men.
text.NABRE: 18:13 cinnamon, spice, incense, myrrh, and frankincense; wine, olive oil, fine flour, and wheat; cattle and sheep, horses and chariots, and slaves, that is, human beings.

text.NRSV: “The fruit for which your soul longed has gone from you, and all your dainties and your splendor are lost to you, never to be found again!”
text.Rotherham: And, the fruit of the coveting of thy soul, hath departed from thee, and, all things rich and bright, have perished from thee; and, no more, in anywise, for them, shall they seek.
text.KJV: 18:14 And the fruits that thy soule lusted after, are departed from thee, and all things which were daintie, and goodly, are departed from thee, and thou shalt finde them no more at all.
text.DRV: 18:14 And the apples of the desire of thy soul are departed from thee, and all fat and goodly things are perished from thee, and they shall no more find them.
text.NABRE: 18:14 “The fruit you craved has left you. All your luxury and splendor are gone, never again will one find them.”

text.NRSV: The merchants of these wares, who gained wealth from her, will stand far off, in fear of her torment, weeping and mourning aloud,
text.Rotherham: The merchants of these things, who were enriched by her, afar off, shall stand, because of their fear of her torment, weeping, and grieving,
text.KJV: 18:15 The Merchants of these things which were made riche by her, shall stand afarre off for the feare of her torment, weeping and wailing.
text.DRV: 18:15 The merchants of these things which are made rich, shall stand far from her for fear of her torments, weeping and mourning,
text.NABRE: 18:15 The merchants who deal in these goods, who grew rich from her, will keep their distance for fear of the torment inflicted on her. Weeping and mourning,

text.NRSV: “Alas, alas, the great city, clothed in fine linen, in purple and scarlet, adorned with gold, with jewels, and with pearls!
text.Rotherham: as they say—Alas! alas! the great city! She that was arrayed in fine linen, and purple, and scarlet, and decked with gold, and precious stone, and pearl,—
text.KJV: 18:16 And saying, Alas, alas, that great city, that was clothed in fine linnen, and purple and scarlet, and decked with gold, and pretious stones, and pearles:
text.DRV: 18:16 & saying: Woe, woe, that great city, which was clothed with silk, and purple, and scarlet, and was gilted with gold, and precious stone, and pearls:
text.NABRE: 18:16 they cry out: “Alas, alas, great city, wearing fine linen, purple and scarlet, adorned [in] gold, precious stones, and pearls.

text.NRSV: For in one hour all this wealth has been laid waste.” And all shipmasters and seafarers, sailors and all whose trade is on the sea, stood far off
text.Rotherham: That, in one hour, hath been laid waste, such great wealth as this! And, every pilot, and every passenger, and mariners, and as many as, by the sea, carry on traffic, afar off did stand,
text.KJV: 18:17 For in one houre so great riches is come to nought. And euery shipmaster, and all the company in ships, and sailers, and as many as trade by sea, stood a farre off,
text.DRV: 18:17 because in one hour are so great riches made desolate: and every governor, & every one that saileth into the lake, and the ship-men, and they that work in the sea, stood a far off,
text.NABRE: 18:17 In one hour this great wealth has been ruined.” Every captain of a ship, every traveler at sea, sailors, and seafaring merchants stood at a distance

text.NRSV: and cried out as they saw the smoke of her burning, “What city was like the great city?”
text.Rotherham: and they cried out, seeing the smoke of her burning, saying—What city is like unto the great city?
text.KJV: 18:18 And cryed when they saw the smoke of her burning, saying, What city is like vnto this great citie?
text.DRV: 18:18 and cried seeing the place of her burning, saying: What other is like to this great city?
text.NABRE: 18:18 and cried out when they saw the smoke of her pyre, “What city could compare with the great city?”

text.NRSV: And they threw dust on their heads, as they wept and mourned, crying out, “Alas, alas, the great city, where all who had ships at sea grew rich by her wealth! For in one hour she has been laid waste.”
text.Rotherham: And they cast dust upon their heads, and cried out, weeping and grieving, saying—Alas! alas! the great city! Whereby were made rich all that had ships in the sea, by reason of her costliness,—that, in one hour, she hath been laid waste!
text.KJV: 18:19 And they cast dust on their heads, and cried, weeping, and wailing, saying, Alas, alas, that great citie, wherein were made rich all that had ships in the sea, by reason of her costlinesse, for in one houre is she made desolate.
text.DRV: 18:19 And they threw dust upon their heads, and cried weeping and mourning, saying: Woe, woe, that great city, in the which all were made rich that had ships in the sea, of her prices: because in one hour she is desolate.
text.NABRE: 18:19 They threw dust on their heads and cried out, weeping and mourning: “Alas, alas, great city, in which all who had ships at sea grew rich from her wealth. In one hour she has been ruined.

text.NRSV: Rejoice over her, O heaven: you saints and apostles and prophets! For God has given judgment for you against her.
text.Rotherham: Be glad over her, thou heaven! And ye saints, and ye apostles, and ye prophets! For that God hath exacted your vindication from her.
text.KJV: 18:20 Reioyce ouer her thou heauen, and ye holy Apostles and Prophets, for God hath auenged you on her.
text.DRV: 18:20 Rejoice over her, heaven, and ye holy Apostles and Prophets: because God hath judged your judgement of her.
text.NABRE: 18:20 Rejoice over her, heaven, you holy ones, apostles, and prophets. For God has judged your case against her.”

text.NRSV: Then a mighty angel took up a stone like a great millstone and threw it into the sea, saying, “With such violence Babylon the great city will be thrown down, and will be found no more;
text.Rotherham: And one mighty messenger lifted a stone, as it were a great millstone, and cast it into the sea, saying—Thus, with main force, shall be cast down, Babylon the great city,—and in nowise be found any more;
text.KJV: 18:21 And a mightie Angel tooke vp a stone like a great milstone, and cast it into the sea, saying, Thus with violence shall that great citie Babylon bee throwen downe, and shall bee found no more at all.
text.DRV: 18:21 And one strong Angel took up as it were a great millstone, and threw it into the sea, saying: With this violence shall Babylon that great city be thrown, and shall now be found no more.
text.NABRE: 18:21 A mighty angel picked up a stone like a huge millstone and threw it into the sea and said: “With such force will Babylon the great city be thrown down, and will never be found again.

text.NRSV: and the sound of harpists and minstrels and of flutists and trumpeters will be heard in you no more; and an artisan of any trade will be found in you no more; and the sound of the millstone will be heard in you no more;
text.Rotherham: And sound of harp-singers, and musicians, and flute-players, and trumpeters,—in nowise be heard in thee any more; and any artisan [of any art],—in nowise be found in thee any more; and sound of millstone,—in nowise be found in thee any more;
text.KJV: 18:22 And the voyce of harpers and musitions, and of pipers, and trumpetters, shall bee heard no more at all in thee: and no craftsman, of whatsoeuer craft hee be, shall be found any more in thee: and the sound of a milstone shalbe heard no more at all in thee:
text.DRV: 18:22 And the voice of harpers, and of Musicians, and of them that sing on shalm and trumpet, shall no more be heard in thee, & every artificer of every art shall be found no more in thee, and the noise of the mill shall no more be heard in thee,
text.NABRE: 18:22 No melodies of harpists and musicians, flutists and trumpeters, will ever be heard in you again. No craftsmen in any trade will ever be found in you again. No sound of the millstone will ever be heard in you again.

text.NRSV: and the light of a lamp will shine in you no more; and the voice of bridegroom and bride will be heard in you no more; for your merchants were the magnates of the earth, and all nations were deceived by your sorcery.
text.Rotherham: And light of lamp,—in nowise shine in thee any more; and voice of bridegroom and bride,—in nowise be heard in thee any more; because, thy merchants, were the great ones of the earth, because, with thy sorcery, were all the nations deceived:
text.KJV: 18:23 And the light of a candle shall shine no more at all in thee: and the voice of the bridegrome and of the bride shalbe heard no more at all in thee: for thy Merchants were the great men of the earth: for by thy sorceries were all nations deceiued.
text.DRV: 18:23 and the light of the lamp shall no more shine in thee, & the voice of the bridegroom and the bride shall no more be heard in thee: because thy merchants were the princes of the earth, because all nations have erred in thine enchantments.
text.NABRE: 18:23 No light from a lamp will ever be seen in you again. No voices of bride and groom will ever be heard in you again. Because your merchants were the great ones of the world, all nations were led astray by your magic potion.

text.NRSV: And in her was found the blood of prophets and of saints, and of all who have been slaughtered on earth.”
text.Rotherham: And, in her, blood of prophets and saints, was found,—and of all who had been slain upon the earth.
text.KJV: 18:24 And in her was found the blood of Prophets, and of Saints, and of all that were slaine vpon the earth.
text.DRV: 18:24 And in her is found the blood of the Prophets and Saints, and of all that were slain in the earth.
text.NABRE: 18:24 In her was found the blood of prophets and holy ones and all who have been slain on the earth.”

text.NRSV: After this I heard what seemed to be the loud voice of a great multitude in heaven, saying, “Hallelujah! Salvation and glory and power belong to our God,
text.Rotherham: After these things, I heard as it were, a loud voice of a great multitude in heaven, saying—Hallelujah! The salvation and the glory and the power of our God!
text.KJV: 19:1 And after these things I heard a great voyce of much people in heauen, saying, Alleluia: saluation, and glorie, and honour, and power vnto the Lord our God:
text.DRV: 19:1 After these things I heard as it were the voice of many multitudes in heaven saying, Allelu-ia. Praise, and glory and power is to our God:
text.NABRE: 19:1 Chapter 19 - After this I heard what sounded like the loud voice of a great multitude in heaven, saying: “Alleluia! Salvation, glory, and might belong to our God,

text.NRSV: for his judgments are true and just; he has judged the great whore who corrupted the earth with her fornication, and he has avenged on her the blood of his servants.”
text.Rotherham: Because, true and righteous, are his judgments; because he hath judged the great harlot, who, indeed, corrupted the earth with her lewdness,—and hath avenged the blood of his servants, at her hand.
text.KJV: 19:2 For true and righteous are his iudgements, for hee hath iudged the great whore which did corrupt the earth with her fornication, and hath auenged the blood of his seruants at her hand.
text.DRV: 19:2 because true & just are his judgements which hath judged of the great harlot, that hath corrupted the earth in her whoredom, and hath revenged the blood of his servants, of her hands.
text.NABRE: 19:2 for true and just are his judgments. He has condemned the great harlot who corrupted the earth with her harlotry. He has avenged on her the blood of his servants.”

text.NRSV: Once more they said, “Hallelujah! The smoke from her goes up forever and ever.”
text.Rotherham: And, a second time, have they said—Hallelujah! And, her smoke, ascendeth unto ages of ages.
text.KJV: 19:3 And againe they sayd, Alleluia: and her smoke rose vp for euer & euer.
text.DRV: 19:3 And again they said, Allelu-ia. And her smoke ascendeth for ever and ever.
text.NABRE: 19:3 They said a second time: “Alleluia! Smoke will rise from her forever and ever.”

text.NRSV: And the twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshiped God who is seated on the throne, saying, “Amen. Hallelujah!”
text.Rotherham: And the twenty-four elders and the four living creatures, fell down, and did homage unto God, who sitteth upon the throne, saying—Amen! Hallelujah!
text.KJV: 19:4 And the foure and twentie Elders, and the foure beasts fell downe, and worshipped God that sate on the throne, saying, Amen, Alleluia.
text.DRV: 19:4 And the four and twenty Seniors fell down, and the four beasts, & adored God sitting upon the throne, saying: Amen, Allelu-ia.
text.NABRE: 19:4 The twenty-four elders and the four living creatures fell down and worshiped God who sat on the throne, saying, “Amen. Alleluia.” The Victory Song.

text.NRSV: And from the throne came a voice saying, “Praise our God, all you his servants, and all who fear him, small and great.”
text.Rotherham: And, a voice, from the throne, came forth, saying—Be giving praise unto our God, all ye his servants, ye that revere him, the small and the great.
text.KJV: 19:5 And a voice came out of the throne, saying, Praise our God all yee his seruants, and ye that feare him, both small and great.
text.DRV: 19:5 And a voice came out from the throne, saying: Say praise to our God all ye his servants: and you that fear him, little and great.
text.NABRE: 19:5 A voice coming from the throne said: “Praise our God, all you his servants, [and] you who revere him, small and great.”

text.NRSV: Then I heard what seemed to be the voice of a great multitude, like the sound of many waters and like the sound of mighty thunderpeals, crying out, “Hallelujah! For the Lord our God the Almighty reigns.
text.Rotherham: And I heard as a voice of a great multitude, and as a voice of many waters, and as a voice of mighty thunderings, saying—Hallelujah! Because the Lord, [our] God, the Almighty, hath become king.
text.KJV: 19:6 And I heard as it were the voice of a great multitude, and as the voice of many waters, and as the voice of mightie thundrings, saying, Alleluia: for the Lord God omnipotent reigneth.
text.DRV: 19:6 And I heard as it were the voice of a great multitude, and as the voice of many waters, & as the voice of great thunders, saying, Allelu-ia: because our Lord God the omnipotent hath reigned.
text.NABRE: 19:6 Then I heard something like the sound of a great multitude or the sound of rushing water or mighty peals of thunder, as they said: “Alleluia! The Lord has established his reign, [our] God, the almighty.

text.NRSV: Let us rejoice and exult and give him the glory, for the marriage of the Lamb has come, and his bride has made herself ready;
text.Rotherham: Let us rejoice and exult, and give glory unto him, because the marriage of the Lamb, is come, and, his wife, hath made herself ready;
text.KJV: 19:7 Let vs bee glad and reioyce, and giue honour to him: for the mariage of the Lambe is come, and his wife hath made herselfe readie.
text.DRV: 19:7 Let us be glad and rejoice, and give glory to him: because the marriage of the Lamb is come, & his wife hath prepared herself.
text.NABRE: 19:7 Let us rejoice and be glad and give him glory. For the wedding day of the Lamb has come, his bride has made herself ready.

text.NRSV: to her it has been granted to be clothed with fine linen, bright and pure”— for the fine linen is the righteous deeds of the saints.
text.Rotherham: and it hath been given unto her, that she should be arrayed in fine linen, bright, pure, for, the fine linen, is, the righteous acts of the saints.
text.KJV: 19:8 And to her was granted, that she should bee arayed in fine linnen, cleane and white: for the fine linnen is the righteousnesse of Saints.
text.DRV: 19:8 And it was given to her that she clothe herself with silk glittering and white. For the silk are the justifications of Saints.
text.NABRE: 19:8 She was allowed to wear a bright, clean linen garment.” (The linen represents the righteous deeds of the holy ones.)

text.NRSV: And the angel said to me, “Write this: Blessed are those who are invited to the marriage supper of the Lamb.” And he said to me, “These are true words of God.”
text.Rotherham: And he saith unto me—Write! Happy, they who, unto the marriage supper of the Lamb, have been bidden! And he saith unto me—These words, are, true [words] of God.
text.KJV: 19:9 And hee saith vnto mee, Write, Blessed are they which are called vnto the marriage supper of the Lambe. And he saith vnto mee, These are the true sayings of God.
text.DRV: 19:9 And he said to me: Write, Blessed be they that are called to the supper of the marriage of the Lamb. And he said to me: These words of God, be true.
text.NABRE: 19:9 Then the angel said to me, “Write this: Blessed are those who have been called to the wedding feast of the Lamb.” And he said to me, “These words are true; they come from God.”

text.NRSV: Then I fell down at his feet to worship him, but he said to me, “You must not do that! I am a fellow servant with you and your comrades who hold the testimony of Jesus. Worship God! For the testimony of Jesus is the spirit of prophecy.”
text.Rotherham: And I fell down at his feet, to do him homage; and he saith unto me—See! [thou do it] not! A fellow-servant, am I, of thee and of thy brethren who have the witness of Jesus: unto God, do homage! For, the witness of Jesus, is the spirit of the prophecy.
text.KJV: 19:10 And I fell at his feete to worship him: And he said vnto me, See thou doe it not: I am thy fellow seruant, and of thy brethren, that haue the testimonie of Iesus, Worship God: for the testimony of Iesus, is the spirit of prophecie.
text.DRV: 19:10 And I fell before his feet, to adore him. And he saith to me: See thou do not; I am thy fellow-servant, and of thy brethren that have the testimony of Jesus. Adore God. For the testimony of Jesus, is the spirit of prophecy.
text.NABRE: 19:10 I fell at his feet to worship him. But he said to me, “Don’t! I am a fellow servant of yours and of your brothers who bear witness to Jesus. Worship God. Witness to Jesus is the spirit of prophecy.”

text.NRSV: Then I saw heaven opened, and there was a white horse! Its rider is called Faithful and True, and in righteousness he judges and makes war.
text.Rotherham: And I saw heaven, set open, and lo! a white horse, and, he that was sitting thereon, [called] Faithful, and True; and, in righteousness, doth he judge and make war;
text.KJV: 19:11 And I sawe heauen opened, and behold a white horse, and hee that sate vpon him was called faithful and true, and in righteousnes hee doth iudge and make warre.
text.DRV: 19:11 And I saw heaven opened, and behold a white horse: and he that sat upon him, was called Faithful and True, and with justice he judgeth & fighteth.
text.NABRE: 19:11 The King of Kings. Then I saw the heavens opened, and there was a white horse; its rider was [called] “Faithful and True.” He judges and wages war in righteousness.

text.NRSV: His eyes are like a flame of fire, and on his head are many diadems; and he has a name inscribed that no one knows but himself.
text.Rotherham: and, his eyes, are a flame of fire, and, upon his head, are many diadems, having, a name, written, which, no one, knoweth, but himself,
text.KJV: 19:12 His eyes were as a flame of fire, and on his head were many crownes, and hee had a name written, that no man knew but he himselfe.
text.DRV: 19:12 And his eyes as a flame of fire, and on his head many diadems, having a name written, which no man knoweth but himself.
text.NABRE: 19:12 His eyes were [like] a fiery flame, and on his head were many diadems. He had a name inscribed that no one knows except himself.

text.NRSV: He is clothed in a robe dipped in blood, and his name is called The Word of God.
text.Rotherham: and arrayed with a mantle sprinkled with blood, and his name hath been called—The Word of God.
text.KJV: 19:13 And hee was clothed with a vesture dipt in blood, and his name is called, The word of God.
text.DRV: 19:13 And he was clothed with a garment sprinkled with blood: and his name is called, The Word of God.
text.NABRE: 19:13 He wore a cloak that had been dipped in blood, and his name was called the Word of God.

text.NRSV: And the armies of heaven, wearing fine linen, white and pure, were following him on white horses.
text.Rotherham: And, the armies which were in heaven, were following him, upon white horses, clothed with fine linen, white, pure;
text.KJV: 19:14 And the armies which were in heauen followed him vpon white horses, clothed in fine linnen, white and cleane.
text.DRV: 19:14 And the hosts that are in heaven followed him on white horses clothed in white and pure silk.
text.NABRE: 19:14 The armies of heaven followed him, mounted on white horses and wearing clean white linen.

text.NRSV: From his mouth comes a sharp sword with which to strike down the nations, and he will rule them with a rod of iron; he will tread the winepress of the fury of the wrath of God the Almighty.
text.Rotherham: and, out of his mouth, is going forth a sharp sword, that, therewith, he may smite the nations,—and, he, shall shepherd them with a sceptre of iron, and, he, treadeth the wine-press of the wrath of the anger of God the Almighty.
text.KJV: 19:15 And out of his mouth goeth a sharpe sword, that with it hee should smite the nations: and he shal rule them with a rod of yron: and he treadeth the winepresse of the fiercenesse and wrath of Almighty God.
text.DRV: 19:15 And out of his mouth proceedeth a sharp sword: that in it he may strike the Gentiles. And he shall rule them in a rod of iron: and he treadeth the wine press of the fury of the wrath of God omnipotent.
text.NABRE: 19:15 Out of his mouth came a sharp sword to strike the nations. He will rule them with an iron rod, and he himself will tread out in the wine press the wine of the fury and wrath of God the almighty.

text.NRSV: On his robe and on his thigh he has a name inscribed, “King of kings and Lord of lords.”
text.Rotherham: And he hath, upon his mantle and upon his thigh, a name, written—King of kings, and Lord of lords.
text.KJV: 19:16 And he hath on his vesture, and on his thigh a name written, King Of Kings, And Lord Of Lords.
text.DRV: 19:16 And he hath in his garment and in his thigh written, King of Kings And Lord of Lords.
text.NABRE: 19:16 He has a name written on his cloak and on his thigh, “King of kings and Lord of lords.”

text.NRSV: Then I saw an angel standing in the sun, and with a loud voice he called to all the birds that fly in midheaven, “Come, gather for the great supper of God,
text.Rotherham: And I saw one messenger, standing in the sun, and he cried out with a loud voice, saying, unto all the birds that fly in mid-heaven,—Hither! be gathered together unto the great supper of God,—
text.KJV: 19:17 And I saw an Angel standing in the Sunne, and hee cried with a lowd voyce, saying to all the foules that flie in the midst of heauen, Come and gather your selues together vnto the supper of the great God:
text.DRV: 19:17 And I saw one Angel standing in the sun, & he cried with a loud voice saying to all the birds that did fly by the midst of heaven: Come and assemble together to the great supper of God:
text.NABRE: 19:17 Then I saw an angel standing on the sun. He cried out [in] a loud voice to all the birds flying high overhead, “Come here. Gather for God’s great feast,

text.NRSV: to eat the flesh of kings, the flesh of captains, the flesh of the mighty, the flesh of horses and their riders—flesh of all, both free and slave, both small and great.”
text.Rotherham: that ye may eat the flesh of kings, and the flesh of captains, and the flesh of mighty men, and the flesh of horses, and of them who sit upon them, and the flesh of all, both free and bond, and small and great.
text.KJV: 19:18 That yee may eate the flesh of Kings, and the flesh of Captaines, and the flesh of mighty men, and the flesh of horses, and of them that sit on them, and the flesh of all men both free and bond, both small and great.
text.DRV: 19:18 that you may eat the flesh of kings, and the flesh of tribunes, and the flesh of valiants, and the flesh of horses and of them that sit on them, & the flesh of all free-men and bond-men, and of little and great.
text.NABRE: 19:18 to eat the flesh of kings, the flesh of military officers, and the flesh of warriors, the flesh of horses and of their riders, and the flesh of all, free and slave, small and great.”

text.NRSV: Then I saw the beast and the kings of the earth with their armies gathered to make war against the rider on the horse and against his army.
text.Rotherham: And I saw the wild-beast, and the kings of the earth, and their armies, gathered together—to make war with him who was sitting upon the horse, and with his army.
text.KJV: 19:19 And I saw the beast, & the Kings of the earth, and their armies gathered together to make warre against him that sate on the horse, and against his armie.
text.DRV: 19:19 And I saw the beast and the kings of the earth, & their armies gathered to make war with him that sat upon the horse and with his army.
text.NABRE: 19:19 Then I saw the beast and the kings of the earth and their armies gathered to fight against the one riding the horse and against his army.

text.NRSV: And the beast was captured, and with it the false prophet who had performed in its presence the signs by which he deceived those who had received the mark of the beast and those who worshiped its image. These two were thrown alive into the lake of fire that burns with sulfur.
text.Rotherham: And the wild-beast, was taken, and, with him, the false prophet who wrought the signs before him, whereby he deceived them who received the mark of the wild-beast and them who were doing homage unto his image,—alive, were they two cast into the lake of fire that burneth with brimstone.
text.KJV: 19:20 And the beast was taken, & with him the false prophet, that wrought miracles before him, with which he deceiued them that had receiued the marke of the beast, and them that worshipped his image. These both were cast aliue into a lake of fire burning with brimstone.
text.DRV: 19:20 And the beast was apprehended, and with him the false-prophet: which wrought signs before him, wherewith he seduced them that took the character of the beast, and that adored his image. These two were cast alive into the pool of fire burning also with brimstone.
text.NABRE: 19:20 The beast was caught and with it the false prophet who had performed in its sight the signs by which he led astray those who had accepted the mark of the beast and those who had worshiped its image. The two were thrown alive into the fiery pool burning with sulfur.

text.NRSV: And the rest were killed by the sword of the rider on the horse, the sword that came from his mouth; and all the birds were gorged with their flesh.
text.Rotherham: And, the rest, were slain with the sword of him that was sitting upon the horse, which went forth out of his mouth, and, all the birds, were filled with their flesh.
text.KJV: 19:21 And the remnant were slain with the sword of him that sate vpon the horse, which sword proceeded out of his mouth: and all the foules were filled with their flesh.
text.DRV: 19:21 And the rest were slain by the sword of him that sitteth upon the horse, which proceedeth out of his mouth: and all the birds were filled with their flesh.
text.NABRE: 19:21 The rest were killed by the sword that came out of the mouth of the one riding the horse, and all the birds gorged themselves on their flesh.

text.NRSV: Then I saw an angel coming down from heaven, holding in his hand the key to the bottomless pit and a great chain.
text.Rotherham: And I saw a messenger, coming down out of heaven, having the key of the abyss, and a great chain upon his hand;
text.KJV: 20:1 And I saw an Angel come down from heauen, hauing the key of the bottomles pit, & a great chaine in his hand.
text.DRV: 20:1 And I saw an Angel descending from heaven, having the key of the bottomless depth, and a great chain in his hand.
text.NABRE: 20:1 Chapter 20 - The Thousand-year Reign. Then I saw an angel come down from heaven, holding in his hand the key to the abyss and a heavy chain.

text.NRSV: He seized the dragon, that ancient serpent, who is the Devil and Satan, and bound him for a thousand years,
text.Rotherham: and he laid hold of the dragon, the ancient serpent, who is Adversary and the Accuser, and bound him for a thousand years,—
text.KJV: 20:2 And hee laid hold on the dragon that old serpent, which is the deuill and Satan, and bound him a thousand yeres,
text.DRV: 20:2 And he apprehended the dragon the old serpent, which is the devil and Satan, and bound him for a thousand years.
text.NABRE: 20:2 He seized the dragon, the ancient serpent, which is the Devil or Satan, and tied it up for a thousand years

text.NRSV: and threw him into the pit, and locked and sealed it over him, so that he would deceive the nations no more, until the thousand years were ended. After that he must be let out for a little while.
text.Rotherham: and cast him into the abyss, and fastened and sealed [it] over him,—that he might not deceive the nations any more, until the thousand years, should be ended: after these, must he be loosed for a short time.
text.KJV: 20:3 And cast him into the bottomlesse pit, and shut him vp, and set a seale vpon him, that he should deceiue the nations no more, till the thousand yeeres should bee fulfilled: and after that hee must be loosed a little season.
text.DRV: 20:3 And he threw him into the depth, and shut him up, and sealed over him, that he seduce no more the nations, till the thousand years be consummate. And after these things he must be loosed a little time.
text.NABRE: 20:3 and threw it into the abyss, which he locked over it and sealed, so that it could no longer lead the nations astray until the thousand years are completed. After this, it is to be released for a short time.

text.NRSV: Then I saw thrones, and those seated on them were given authority to judge. I also saw the souls of those who had been beheaded for their testimony to Jesus and for the word of God. They had not worshiped the beast or its image and had not received its mark on their foreheads or their hands. They came to life and reigned with Christ a thousand years.
text.Rotherham: And I saw thrones, and they sat upon them, and sentence of judgment was given unto them; and [I saw] the souls of them who had been beheaded because of the witness of Jesus and because of the word of God, and such as had not done homage unto the wild-beast or unto his image, nor had received the mark upon their forehead or upon their hand; and they lived and reigned with the Christ, for a thousand years.
text.KJV: 20:4 And I saw thrones, and they sate vpon them, and iudgement was giuen vnto them: & I saw the soules of them that were beheaded for the witnesse of Iesus, and for the word of God, and which had not worshipped the beast, neither his image, neither had receiued his marke vpon their foreheads, or in their hands; and they liued and reigned with Christ a thousand yeeres.
text.DRV: 20:4 And I saw seats: and they sat upon them, and judgement was given them, and the souls of the beheaded for the testimony of Jesus, and for the word of God, and that adored not the beast, nor his image, nor received his character in their foreheads or in their hands, and have lived, & reigned with Christ a thousand years.
text.NABRE: 20:4 Then I saw thrones; those who sat on them were entrusted with judgment. I also saw the souls of those who had been beheaded for their witness to Jesus and for the word of God, and who had not worshiped the beast or its image nor had accepted its mark on their foreheads or hands. They came to life and they reigned with Christ for a thousand years.

text.NRSV: (The rest of the dead did not come to life until the thousand years were ended.) This is the first resurrection.
text.Rotherham: The rest of the dead, lived not, until the thousand years, were ended. This, is the first resurrection.
text.KJV: 20:5 But the rest of the dead liued not againe vntill the thousand yeeres were finished. This is the first resurrection.
text.DRV: 20:5 The rest of the dead lived not, till the thousand years be consummate. This is the first resurrection.
text.NABRE: 20:5 The rest of the dead did not come to life until the thousand years were over. This is the first resurrection.

text.NRSV: Blessed and holy are those who share in the first resurrection; over these the second death has no power, but they will be priests of God and of Christ, and they will reign with him a thousand years.
text.Rotherham: Happy and holy, is he that hath part in the first resurrection: upon these, the second death, hath no authority; but they shall be priests of God and of the Christ, and shall reign with him for the thousand years.
text.KJV: 20:6 Blessed & holy is he that hath part in þ first resurrection: on such the second death hath no power, but they shall be Priests of God, and of Christ, and shall reigne with him a thousand yeeres.
text.DRV: 20:6 Blessed and holy is he that hath part in the first resurrection. In these the second death hath not power: but they shall be Priests of God and of Christ: and shall reign with him a thousand years.
text.NABRE: 20:6 Blessed and holy is the one who shares in the first resurrection. The second death has no power over these; they will be priests of God and of Christ, and they will reign with him for [the] thousand years.

text.NRSV: When the thousand years are ended, Satan will be released from his prison
text.Rotherham: And, as soon as the thousand years, shall be ended, the Accuser shall be loosed out of his prison,
text.KJV: 20:7 And when the thousand yeeres are expired, Satan shall be loosed out of his prison,
text.DRV: 20:7 And when the thousand years shall be consummate, Satan shall be loosed out of his prison, and shall go forth and seduce the nations that are upon the four corners of the earth, Gog, and Magog, and shall gather them into battle, the number of whom is as the sand of the sea.
text.NABRE: 20:7 When the thousand years are completed, Satan will be released from his prison.

text.NRSV: and will come out to deceive the nations at the four corners of the earth, Gog and Magog, in order to gather them for battle; they are as numerous as the sands of the sea.
text.Rotherham: and will go forth to deceive the nations that are in the four corners of the earth, the Gog and Magog, to gather them together unto the battle—the number of whom, is as the sand of the sea.
text.KJV: 20:8 And shall goe out to deceiue the nations which are in the foure quarters of the earth, Gog & Magog, to gather them together to battell: the number of whom is as the sand of the sea.
text.DRV: 20:8 And they ascended upon the breadth of the earth, and compassed the camp of the Saints, and the beloved city.
text.NABRE: 20:8 He will go out to deceive the nations at the four corners of the earth, Gog and Magog, to gather them for battle; their number is like the sand of the sea.

text.NRSV: They marched up over the breadth of the earth and surrounded the camp of the saints and the beloved city. And fire came down from heaven and consumed them.
text.Rotherham: And they came up over the breadth of the land, and surrounded the camp of the saints, and the beloved city. And there came down fire out of heaven, and devoured them;
text.KJV: 20:9 And they went vp on the breadth of the earth, and compassed the campe of the Saints about, and the beloued citie: and fire came downe from God out of heauen, and deuoured them.
text.DRV: 20:9 And there came down fire from God out of heaven, and devoured them:
text.NABRE: 20:9 They invaded the breadth of the earth and surrounded the camp of the holy ones and the beloved city. But fire came down from heaven and consumed them.

text.NRSV: And the devil who had deceived them was thrown into the lake of fire and sulfur, where the beast and the false prophet were, and they will be tormented day and night forever and ever.
text.Rotherham: and, the Adversary that had been deceiving them, was cast into the lake of fire and brimstone, where [were] both the wild-beast and the false-prophet; and they shall be tormented, day and night, unto the ages of ages.
text.KJV: 20:10 And the deuil that deceiued them, was cast into the lake of fire and brimstone, where the beast and the false prophet are, and shall be tormented day and night, for euer and euer.
text.DRV: 20:10 and the devil which seduced them, was cast into the pool of fire and brimstone, where both the beast & the false-prophet shall be tormented day and night for ever and ever.
text.NABRE: 20:10 The Devil who had led them astray was thrown into the pool of fire and sulfur, where the beast and the false prophet were. There they will be tormented day and night forever and ever. The Large White Throne.

text.NRSV: Then I saw a great white throne and the one who sat on it; the earth and the heaven fled from his presence, and no place was found for them.
text.Rotherham: And I saw a great white throne, and him that was sitting thereon, from whose face fled the earth and heaven, and, place, was not found for them.
text.KJV: 20:11 And I saw a great white throne, and him that sate on it, from whose face the earth and the heauen fled away, and there was found no place for them.
text.DRV: 20:11 And I saw a great white throne, and one sitting upon it, from whose sight earth and heaven fled, and there was no place found for them.
text.NABRE: 20:11 Next I saw a large white throne and the one who was sitting on it. The earth and the sky fled from his presence and there was no place for them.

text.NRSV: And I saw the dead, great and small, standing before the throne, and books were opened. Also another book was opened, the book of life. And the dead were judged according to their works, as recorded in the books.
text.Rotherham: And I saw the dead, the great and the small, standing before the throne; and, books, were opened, and, another book, was opened, which is, the book, of life; and the dead, were judged, out of the things written in the books, according to their works.
text.KJV: 20:12 And I sawe the dead, small and great, stand before God: and the books were opened: & another booke was opened, which is the booke of life: and the dead were iudged out of those things which were written in the books, according to their works.
text.DRV: 20:12 And I saw the dead, great and little, standing in the sight of the throne, and books were opened: and another book was opened, which is of life: and the dead were judged of those things which were written in the books according to their works.
text.NABRE: 20:12 I saw the dead, the great and the lowly, standing before the throne, and scrolls were opened. Then another scroll was opened, the book of life. The dead were judged according to their deeds, by what was written in the scrolls.

text.NRSV: And the sea gave up the dead that were in it, Death and Hades gave up the dead that were in them, and all were judged according to what they had done.
text.Rotherham: And the sea gave up the dead that were in it, and, death and hades, gave up the dead that were in them; and they were judged, each one, according to their works.
text.KJV: 20:13 And the sea gaue vp the dead which were in it: and death and hell deliuered vp the dead which were in them: and they were iudged euery man according to their works.
text.DRV: 20:13 And the sea gave the dead that were in it, and death and hell gave their dead that were in them, and it was judged of every one according to their works.
text.NABRE: 20:13 The sea gave up its dead; then Death and Hades gave up their dead. All the dead were judged according to their deeds.

text.NRSV: Then Death and Hades were thrown into the lake of fire. This is the second death, the lake of fire;
text.Rotherham: And, death and hades, were cast into the lake of fire. This, is, the second death—the lake of fire.
text.KJV: 20:14 And death and hell were cast into the lake of fire: this is the second death.
text.DRV: 20:14 And hell and death were cast into the pool of fire. This is the second death.
text.NABRE: 20:14 Then Death and Hades were thrown into the pool of fire. (This pool of fire is the second death.)

text.NRSV: and anyone whose name was not found written in the book of life was thrown into the lake of fire.
text.Rotherham: And, if anyone was not found, in the book of life, written, he was cast into the lake of fire.
text.KJV: 20:15 And whosoeuer was not found written in the booke of life, was cast into the lake of fire.
text.DRV: 20:15 And he that was not found written in the book of life, was cast into the pool of fire.
text.NABRE: 20:15 Anyone whose name was not found written in the book of life was thrown into the pool of fire.

text.NRSV: Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth; for the first heaven and the first earth had passed away, and the sea was no more.
text.Rotherham: And I saw a new heaven and a new earth; for, the first heaven and the first earth, have passed away, and, the sea, is no more.
text.KJV: 21:1 And I saw a new heauen, and a new earth: for the first heauen, and the first earth were passed away, and there was no more sea.
text.DRV: 21:1 And I saw a new heaven and a new earth. For the first heaven, and the first earth was gone, & the sea now is not.
text.NABRE: 21:1 VI. The New Creation Chapter 21 - The New Heaven and the New Earth. Then I saw a new heaven and a new earth. The former heaven and the former earth had passed away, and the sea was no more.

text.NRSV: And I saw the holy city, the new Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.
text.Rotherham: And, the holy city, new Jerusalem, saw I coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.
text.KJV: 21:2 And I Iohn saw the holy City, new Hierusalem comming down from God out of heauen, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.
text.DRV: 21:2 And I John saw the holy city Jerusalem new descending from heaven, prepared of God, as a bride adorned for her husband.
text.NABRE: 21:2 I also saw the holy city, a new Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God, prepared as a bride adorned for her husband.

text.NRSV: And I heard a loud voice from the throne saying, “See, the home of God is among mortals. He will dwell with them; they will be his peoples, and God himself will be with them;
text.Rotherham: And I heard a loud voice out of the throne, saying—Lo! the tent of God, is with men, and he will tabernacle with them, and, they, shall be, his peoples, and, he, shall be, God with them;
text.KJV: 21:3 And I heard a great voice out of heauen, saying, Behold, the Tabernacle of God is with men, and he wil dwell with them, and they shall be his people, and God himselfe shalbe with them, and be their God.
text.DRV: 21:3 And I heard a loud voice from the throne saying: Behold the tabernacle of God with men, and he will dwell with them. And they shall be his people: and he God with them shall be their God.
text.NABRE: 21:3 I heard a loud voice from the throne saying, “Behold, God’s dwelling is with the human race. He will dwell with them and they will be his people and God himself will always be with them [as their God].

text.NRSV: he will wipe every tear from their eyes. Death will be no more; mourning and crying and pain will be no more, for the first things have passed away.”
text.Rotherham: And he will wipe away every tear out of their eyes,—and, death, shall be no more, and grief and outcry and pain shall be no more: the first things, have passed away.
text.KJV: 21:4 And God shall wipe away all teares from their eyes: and there shall bee no more death, neither sorrow, nor crying, neither shall there bee any more paine: for the former things are passed away.
text.DRV: 21:4 And God shall wipe away all tears from their eyes: and death shall be no more, nor mourning, nor crying, neither shall there be sorrow any more, which first things, are gone.
text.NABRE: 21:4 He will wipe every tear from their eyes, and there shall be no more death or mourning, wailing or pain, [for] the old order has passed away.”

text.NRSV: And the one who was seated on the throne said, “See, I am making all things new.” Also he said, “Write this, for these words are trustworthy and true.”
text.Rotherham: And he that was sitting upon the throne said— Lo! I make all things, new. And he saith—Write! because, these words, are, faithful and true.
text.KJV: 21:5 And he that sate vpon the throne, said, Behold, I make all things new. And hee said vnto me, Write: for these words are true and faithfull.
text.DRV: 21:5 And he that sat in the throne, said: Behold I make all things new. And he said to me: Write, because these words be most faithful and true.
text.NABRE: 21:5 The one who sat on the throne said, “Behold, I make all things new.” Then he said, “Write these words down, for they are trustworthy and true.”

text.NRSV: Then he said to me, “It is done! I am the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the thirsty I will give water as a gift from the spring of the water of life.
text.Rotherham: And he said unto me—Accomplished! I, am the A, and the Z, the Beginning and the End: I, unto him that is thirsting, will give of the fountain of the water of life, freely:
text.KJV: 21:6 And he said vnto mee, It is done: I am Alpha and Omega, the beginning and the end. I will giue vnto him that is athirst, of the fountaine of the water of life, freely.
text.DRV: 21:6 And he said to me: It is done, I am Alpha and Omega: the beginning and the end. To him that thirsteth I will give of the fountain of the water of life, gratis.
text.NABRE: 21:6 He said to me, “They are accomplished. I [am] the Alpha and the Omega, the beginning and the end. To the thirsty I will give a gift from the spring of life-giving water.

text.NRSV: Those who conquer will inherit these things, and I will be their God and they will be my children.
text.Rotherham: He that overcometh, shall inherit these things,—And I will be, to him, a God, and, he, shall be, to me, a son;
text.KJV: 21:7 He that ouercommeth, shall inherite all things, and I will bee his God, and he shall be my sonne.
text.DRV: 21:7 He that shall overcome, shall possess these things, and I will be his God: and he shall be my son.
text.NABRE: 21:7 The victor will inherit these gifts, and I shall be his God, and he will be my son.

text.NRSV: But as for the cowardly, the faithless, the polluted, the murderers, the fornicators, the sorcerers, the idolaters, and all liars, their place will be in the lake that burns with fire and sulfur, which is the second death.”
text.Rotherham: But, as for the timid, and disbelieving, and abominable, and murderers, and fornicators, and sorcerers, and idolaters, and all the false, their part, is in the lake that burneth with fire and brimstone,—which is the second death.
text.KJV: 21:8 But the fearefull, and vnbeleeuing, and the abominable, and murderers, and whore mongers, and sorcerers, and idolaters, and all lyars, shall haue their part in the lake which burneth with fire and brimstone: which is the second death.
text.DRV: 21:8 But to the fearful, and incredulous, and execrable, and murderers, and fornicators, and sorcerers, and idolaters, and all liars, their part shall be in the pool burning with fire and brimstone, which is the second death.
text.NABRE: 21:8 But as for cowards, the unfaithful, the depraved, murderers, the unchaste, sorcerers, idol-worshipers, and deceivers of every sort, their lot is in the burning pool of fire and sulfur, which is the second death.” The New Jerusalem.

text.NRSV: Then one of the seven angels who had the seven bowls full of the seven last plagues came and said to me, “Come, I will show you the bride, the wife of the Lamb.”
text.Rotherham: And there came one of the seven messengers which had the seven bowls, that were full of the seven last plagues, and spake with me, saying— Hither! I will point out to thee the bride, the wife of the Lamb.
text.KJV: 21:9 And there came vnto me one of the seuen Angels, which had the seuen vials full of the seuen last plagues, and talked with me, saying, Come hither, I will shew thee the Bride, the Lambes wife.
text.DRV: 21:9 And there came one of the seven Angels that had the vials full of the seven last plagues, and spake with me, saying: Come, and I will shew thee the bride, the wife of the Lamb.
text.NABRE: 21:9 One of the seven angels who held the seven bowls filled with the seven last plagues came and said to me, “Come here. I will show you the bride, the wife of the Lamb.”

text.NRSV: And in the spirit he carried me away to a great, high mountain and showed me the holy city Jerusalem coming down out of heaven from God.
text.Rotherham: And he carried me away, in spirit, unto a mountain great and high, and pointed out to me the holy city, Jerusalem, coming down out of heaven from God;
text.KJV: 21:10 And he caried me away in the spirit to a great and high mountaine, and shewed me that great citie, the holy Hierusalem, descending out of heauen from God,
text.DRV: 21:10 And he took me up in spirit unto a mountain great and high: and he shewed me the holy city Jerusalem descending out of heaven from God,
text.NABRE: 21:10 He took me in spirit to a great, high mountain and showed me the holy city Jerusalem coming down out of heaven from God.

text.NRSV: It has the glory of God and a radiance like a very rare jewel, like jasper, clear as crystal.
text.Rotherham: having the glory of God,—her lustre, like unto a stone most precious, as a jasper stone, shining as crystal;
text.KJV: 21:11 Hauing the glory of God: and her light was like vnto a stone most precious; euen like a iasper stone, cleare as christal,
text.DRV: 21:11 having the glory of God, and the light thereof like to a precious stone, as it were to the jasper stone, even as crystal.
text.NABRE: 21:11 It gleamed with the splendor of God. Its radiance was like that of a precious stone, like jasper, clear as crystal.

text.NRSV: It has a great, high wall with twelve gates, and at the gates twelve angels, and on the gates are inscribed the names of the twelve tribes of the Israelites;
text.Rotherham: having a wall great and high, having twelve gates, and, at the gates, twelve messengers, and names inscribed, which are [the names] of the twelve tribes of the sons of Israel,—
text.KJV: 21:12 And had a wall great and high, and had twelue gates, and at the gates twelue Angels, & names written thereon, which are the names of the twelue tribes of the children of Israel.
text.DRV: 21:12 And it had a wall great and high, having twelve gates, and in the gates twelve Angels, & names written thereon, which are the names of the twelve tribes of the children of Israel.
text.NABRE: 21:12 It had a massive, high wall, with twelve gates where twelve angels were stationed and on which names were inscribed, [the names] of the twelve tribes of the Israelites.

text.NRSV: on the east three gates, on the north three gates, on the south three gates, and on the west three gates.
text.Rotherham: on the east, three gates, and, on the north, three gates, and, on the south, three gates, and, on the west, three gates;
text.KJV: 21:13 On the East three gates, on the North three gates, on the South three gates, and on the West three gates.
text.DRV: 21:13 On the East, three gates: and on the North, three gates: and on the South, three gates: and on the West, three gates.
text.NABRE: 21:13 There were three gates facing east, three north, three south, and three west.

text.NRSV: And the wall of the city has twelve foundations, and on them are the twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
text.Rotherham: and, the wall of the city, having twelve foundations, and, upon them, twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.
text.KJV: 21:14 And the wall of the citie had twelue foundations, and in them the names of the twelue Apostles of the Lambe.
text.DRV: 21:14 And the wall of the city having twelve foundations: and in them, twelve names, of the twelve Apostles of the Lamb.
text.NABRE: 21:14 The wall of the city had twelve courses of stones as its foundation, on which were inscribed the twelve names of the twelve apostles of the Lamb.

text.NRSV: The angel who talked to me had a measuring rod of gold to measure the city and its gates and walls.
text.Rotherham: And, he that was talking with me, had, for a measure, a reed of gold, that he might measure the city, and the gates thereof, and the wall thereof.
text.KJV: 21:15 And hee that talked with mee, had a golden reede to measure the citie, and the gates thereof, and the wall thereof.
text.DRV: 21:15 And he that spake with me, had a measure of a reed, of gold, to measure the city and the gates thereof, and the wall.
text.NABRE: 21:15 The one who spoke to me held a gold measuring rod to measure the city, its gates, and its wall.

text.NRSV: The city lies foursquare, its length the same as its width; and he measured the city with his rod, fifteen hundred miles; its length and width and height are equal.
text.Rotherham: And, the city, four-square, lieth, and, the length thereof, is as great as the breadth. And he measured the city, with the reed,—twelve thousand furlongs: the length, and the breadth, and the height thereof, are, equal.
text.KJV: 21:16 And the city lieth foure square, and the length is as large as the breadth: and he measured the city with the reed, twelue thousand furlongs: the length, and the breadth, and the height of it are equall.
text.DRV: 21:16 And the city is situated quadrangle-wise, and the length thereof is as great as also the breadth: and he measured the city with the reed for twelve thousand furlongs, & the length and height and breadth thereof be equal.
text.NABRE: 21:16 The city was square, its length the same as [also] its width. He measured the city with the rod and found it fifteen hundred miles in length and width and height.

text.NRSV: He also measured its wall, one hundred forty-four cubits by human measurement, which the angel was using.
text.Rotherham: And he measured the wall thereof,—a hundred and forty-four cubits: the measure of a man, which is [the measure] of a messenger.
text.KJV: 21:17 And he measured the wall thereof, an hundred, and fourtie, and foure cubites, according to the measure of a man, that is, of the Angel.
text.DRV: 21:17 And he measured the wall thereof of an hundred forty four cubits, the measure of a man which is of an Angel.
text.NABRE: 21:17 He also measured its wall: one hundred and forty-four cubits according to the standard unit of measurement the angel used.

text.NRSV: The wall is built of jasper, while the city is pure gold, clear as glass.
text.Rotherham: And, the structure of the wall thereof, was jasper, and, the city, was pure gold, like unto pure glass.
text.KJV: 21:18 And the building of the wall of it was of Iasper, and the city was pure gold, like vnto cleare glasse.
text.DRV: 21:18 And the building of the wall thereof was of jasper stone: but the city it-self pure gold, like to pure glass.
text.NABRE: 21:18 The wall was constructed of jasper, while the city was pure gold, clear as glass.

text.NRSV: The foundations of the wall of the city are adorned with every jewel; the first was jasper, the second sapphire, the third agate, the fourth emerald,
text.Rotherham: The foundations of the wall of the city, with every precious stone were adorned: the first foundation, was jasper, the second, sapphire, the third, chalcedony, the fourth, emerald,
text.KJV: 21:19 And the foundations of the wall of the city were garnished with all maner of precious stones. The first foundation was Iasper, the second Saphir, the third a Chalcedony, the fourth an Emerald,
text.DRV: 21:19 And the foundations of the wall of the city, were adorned with all precious stone. The first foundation, the jasper: the second, the sapphire: the third, the calcedonius: the fourth, the emerald:
text.NABRE: 21:19 The foundations of the city wall were decorated with every precious stone; the first course of stones was jasper, the second sapphire, the third chalcedony, the fourth emerald,

text.NRSV: the fifth onyx, the sixth carnelian, the seventh chrysolite, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth chrysoprase, the eleventh jacinth, the twelfth amethyst.
text.Rotherham: the fifth, sardonyx, the sixth, sardius, the seventh, chrysolite, the eighth, beryl, the ninth, topaz, the tenth, chrysoprase, the eleventh, hyacinth, the twelfth, amethyst;
text.KJV: 21:20 The fift Sardonix, the sixt Sardius, the seuenth Chrysolite, the eight Beryl, the ninth a Topas, the tenth a Chrysoprasus, the eleuenth a Iacinct, the twelfth an Amethyst.
text.DRV: 21:20 the fifth, the sardonix: the sixth, the sardius: the seventh, the chrysolithus: the eight, the beryllus: the ninth, the topazius: the tenth, the chrysoprasus: the eleventh, the hyacinth: the twelfth, the amethyst.
text.NABRE: 21:20 the fifth sardonyx, the sixth carnelian, the seventh chrysolite, the eighth beryl, the ninth topaz, the tenth chrysoprase, the eleventh hyacinth, and the twelfth amethyst.

text.NRSV: And the twelve gates are twelve pearls, each of the gates is a single pearl, and the street of the city is pure gold, transparent as glass.
text.Rotherham: and, the twelve gates, were twelve pearls,—each one of the gates, severally, was of one pearl; and, the broadway of the city, was pure gold, as transparent glass.
text.KJV: 21:21 And the twelue gates were twelue pearles, euery seuerall gate was of one pearle, and the streete of the city was pure golde, as it were transparent glasse.
text.DRV: 21:21 And the twelve gates: there are twelve pearls, one to every one: & every gate was of one several pearl. And the street of the city pure gold, as it were transparent glass.
text.NABRE: 21:21 The twelve gates were twelve pearls, each of the gates made from a single pearl; and the street of the city was of pure gold, transparent as glass.

text.NRSV: I saw no temple in the city, for its temple is the Lord God the Almighty and the Lamb.
text.Rotherham: And, sanctuary, saw I none therein; for, the Lord, God, the Almighty, is the sanctuary thereof, and the Lamb.
text.KJV: 21:22 And I saw no Temple therein: For the Lord God Almightie, and the Lambe, are the Temple of it.
text.DRV: 21:22 And temple I saw not therein. For our Lord the God omnipotent is the temple thereof, and the Lamb.
text.NABRE: 21:22 I saw no temple in the city, for its temple is the Lord God almighty and the Lamb.

text.NRSV: And the city has no need of sun or moon to shine on it, for the glory of God is its light, and its lamp is the Lamb.
text.Rotherham: And, the city, hath no need of the sun, nor of the moon, that they should shine therein; for, the glory of God, illumined it, and, the lamp thereof, was the Lamb.
text.KJV: 21:23 And the citie had no need of the Sunne, neither of the Moone to shine in it: for the glory of God did lighten it, and the Lambe is the light thereof.
text.DRV: 21:23 And the city needeth not sun nor moon, to shine in it. For the glory of God hath illuminated it, and the Lamb is the lamp thereof.
text.NABRE: 21:23 The city had no need of sun or moon to shine on it, for the glory of God gave it light, and its lamp was the Lamb.

text.NRSV: The nations will walk by its light, and the kings of the earth will bring their glory into it.
text.Rotherham: And the nations, shall walk, through her light; and, the kings of the earth, do bring their glory into it,
text.KJV: 21:24 And the nations of them which are saued, shall walke in the light of it: and the kings of the earth doe bring their glory and honour into it.
text.DRV: 21:24 And the Gentiles shall walk in the light of it: and the kings of the earth shall bring their glory and honour into it.
text.NABRE: 21:24 The nations will walk by its light, and to it the kings of the earth will bring their treasure.

text.NRSV: Its gates will never be shut by day—and there will be no night there.
text.Rotherham: and, the gates thereof, shall in nowise be shut, by day,—night, in fact, shall not be there,—
text.KJV: 21:25 And the gates of it shall not bee shut at all by day: for there shall bee no night there.
text.DRV: 21:25 And the gates thereof shall not be shut by day: for there shall be no night there.
text.NABRE: 21:25 During the day its gates will never be shut, and there will be no night there.

text.NRSV: People will bring into it the glory and the honor of the nations.
text.Rotherham: and they shall bring the glory and the honour of the nations into it.
text.KJV: 21:26 And they shall bring the glorie and honour of the nations into it.
text.DRV: 21:26 And they shall bring the glory and honour of nations into it.
text.NABRE: 21:26 The treasure and wealth of the nations will be brought there,

text.NRSV: But nothing unclean will enter it, nor anyone who practices abomination or falsehood, but only those who are written in the Lamb’s book of life.
text.Rotherham: And in nowise shall there enter into it, anything common, or he that doeth abomination and falsehood,—but only they who are written in the Lamb’s book of life.
text.KJV: 21:27 And there shall in no wise enter into it any thing that defileth, neither whatsoeuer worketh abomination, or maketh a lie: but they which are written in the Lambes booke of life.
text.DRV: 21:27 There shall not enter into it any polluted thing, nor that doeth abomination and maketh lie, but they that are written in the book of life of the Lamb.
